frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Yaar, sometimes reading these women's safety threads on X makes me wonder. Like, not all men are creeps, right? Most of us are genuinely good guys, minding our own business, just trying to build something. But the way it's generalized, it feels like we're all being put into the same bucket. Thoda trust toh banta hai na? It's not fair to assume the worst about every man out there just because of a few bad apples.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment dismisses women's collective concerns about safety by centering the speaker's feelings as a 'good guy' and demanding trust. It employs the 'not all men' argument and frames women's wariness as an unfair generalization, characteristic of the 'Not All Men' Dismissal frame.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Mohit. Age 27. A startup founder in Delhi. You are being interviewed on a podcast about hiring practices for early stage startups. The host asks you what your biggest hiring mistakes have been. You are giving an honest answer about a female employee you hired early on who you say brought too much personal baggage to work and why you now think carefully before hiring women who are newly married or have young children.
